what_it_does: It is an append-only, peer-to-peer relay log in which multiple AI sessions negotiate the reconciliation of parallel development streams into one main branch, coordinate shared-file edit windows, verify union gates, and later establish a three-way shared-main protocol.
observations: Documents the two-session merge of an interface stream and a cognition stream into main, including a proposed Step A / Step B reconcile and driver/parker assignment.
Records a specific silent-regression catch at suite.py line 3756 and the negotiated thread-scoped fix.
Lists exact verification suites and commands for both sessions, plus post-merge red/green counts and commit hashes.
Tracks the mode-dial-built-twice realization and the co-designed unified MODE_REGISTRY shape.
Evolves into three-way coordination among coherence, cognition, and guided-review sessions, with a CLAIMS board, tiered flags, and a convergence round.
References external coordination artifacts such as build-prep/claude-design/BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md, AUTHORING-FE-HANDOFF.md, and build-prep/coordination/WORK-SPLIT.md.
Includes live operational snapshots: deploy status, suite counts, branch-model decisions, and a structural flag about a red cast_beyond_listening_acceptance suite.
Embodies governance laws like registry-is-truth, reflects-never-owns, propose-not-apply, and a no-hardcoding rule binding all sessions.
standouts: The line-3756 silent-revert catch and the negotiated driver/parker protocol.
The mode-dial-built-twice insight and the unified MODE_REGISTRY co-design.
The shift from two-session merge log to three-session shared-main coordination with CLAIMS windows and tiered flags.
The coherence substrate framed as a third sibling lens and the no-hardcoding governance rule.
conventions: Relay-style, turn-based append-only log with explicit session sign-offs and round markers.
Shared-file window claims posted in the file before editing, especially for suite.py.
Verification-gated commits: company suites, reachability, registry-vs-live must be green before shared-file commits.
Additive, file-disjoint lanes and no winner/ranking language.
Registry-is-truth, reflects-never-owns, and propose-not-apply as standing laws.
Uses commit hashes and suite counts as evidence markers inside a human-readable markdown file.
concerns: Very long and append-only; current state is buried in chronological interleaving.
Many forward-looking HOLD markers and promises may become stale if not updated.
Mixes merge reconciliation, design rationale, operational status, and process protocol without an index.
Some green/red assertions are session-reported and cannot be independently verified from the file alone.
The file outgrew its original merge-coordination scope into a general multi-agent project-management channel.
notes: A living relay log where AI sessions negotiate merge reconciliation, integration seams, shared-file windows, and shared-main discipline.
questions: What are the exact 13 axes in MODE_REGISTRY?
Which 9 files had conflicts and what were the 7 resolved conflict hunks?
What are the full contents of the referenced BACKEND-SEAM-PACK.md, WORK-SPLIT.md, and COHERENCE-SUBSTRATE.md files?
How is the CLAIMS board structured and enforced outside this file?
What is the live deploy status relative to the most recent main commit?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is merge coordination between interface and cognition sessions; actual content expanded into ongoing multi-session project coordination, integration design, and governance protocol.
apparent_themes: Coherence and drift prevention across AI sessions
intention_signals: It tries to prevent collisions and silent regressions when multiple AI sessions build on the same branch by establishing a negotiated driver/parker protocol, shared verification gates, explicit ownership windows, and later scaling that protocol to three concurrent lanes with coherence detection.
novelty: A git-tracked multi-agent coordination transcript used as the primary channel for merge reconciliation and concurrent-build governance, complete with commit-hash evidence, suite-count verification, and a CLAIMS board.
contribution: The documented negotiation of the merge driver/parker, the line-3756 regression catch, the verification gate lists, the mode-dial co-design shape, the three-way CLAIMS/window protocol, and the coherence/no-hardcoding governance rules as institutional memory for concurrent AI building.
summary_for_embedding: MERGE-COORDINATION.md is an append-only, multi-session relay log that records the negotiation, verification, and integration of parallel AI development streams into a shared main branch, later expanding into a three-way coordination protocol with file-claim windows, verification gates, and coherence governance.
intention_for_embedding: It was created so parallel AI sessions could agree on who drives merges, resolve conflicts without collision, verify union gates, and coordinate ongoing shared-file edits.
